summary: Gruber argues that Apple Watch occupies a unique position in Apple's product history: it's the first successful Apple product that isn't the nicest in its category. While Apple Watch dominates by revenue and functionality, traditional luxury watches remain far nicer as physical objects. The Series 4 represents Apple's most significant step toward closing that niceness gap, with a thinner case, larger display, improved haptics, and new watch faces that embrace the hardware rather than hide it. Gruber draws a parallel between Series 4 and iPhone 4 as the generation that elevates the original design to a new level. The review concludes that the higher price is justified — welcome to the world of nicer watches.
key_points: Apple Watch is uniquely anomalous in Apple history: the first hit product that isn't the nicest in its category, succeeding because it does vastly more than traditional watches rather than being more refined

The Series 4's thinner case, larger display, improved Digital Crown haptics, and new colorful watch faces represent the most significant design leap since the original, paralleling what iPhone 4 was to iPhone
Apple's obsessive attention to bands and straps — the Sport Band tuck, tool-free link adjustment, swappable connectors — proves Apple takes watchmaking seriously even where the case can't yet compete
The new full-bleed watch faces fundamentally change Apple Watch's aesthetic from dark backgrounds hiding bezels to colorful designs that celebrate the display's corners
The stainless steel pricing model — identical functionality at hundreds more for nicer materials — makes no sense for computers but perfect sense for watches
money_quotes: What makes Apple Watch different from every other product the company makes, though, is a measure near and dear to the company's soul by which they cannot claim Apple Watch to be number one: nicest.
Apple Watch is thriving despite being far from the nicest watch because all of the watches that are nicer do so much less.
They're winning, but they don't just want to win the race. They want to win the race while driving the best-looking car on the track.

The fundamental problem Apple faces is that the smallest possible computer they can make — today — to do what they want Apple Watch to do isn't small enough to fit it into a watch case of size and shape that would be considered among the very nicest in the world.
Apple Watch's well-liquor straps are far better-designed than the top-shelf-liquor straps from many luxury watchmakers.
Series 4 is to Apple Watch what iPhone 4 was to iPhone — the model that takes the original design to a new level.
Welcome to the world of nicer watches.
It sounds like roll-your-eyes-prima-donna-designer talk, but Apple's faces really are designed hand-in-hand with the hardware.
key_insight: Apple Watch is Apple's first successful product that wins not by being the nicest in its category, but by doing so much more than its nicer competitors — and Series 4 is the generation where Apple begins seriously closing that gap.
